allies.rationale_text: Anchoring helps explain why reference points and initial frames distort how prospect-theory choices are evaluated.
allies.source_quote: This phenomenon also interacts with cognitive biases like Anchoring.

allies.rationale_text: Incentives interact with prospect-theory because gain and loss framing can change behavior without changing the underlying economics.
allies.source_quote: framing outcomes around relative gains or losses (emotional incentives)

antagonists.rationale_text: Critical-thinking counters prospect-theory distortions by forcing a colder review of whether the frame is changing the choice more than the fundamentals are.
antagonists.source_quote: not a plausible normative decision theory

structured_tensions.tension_text: Prospect-theory conflicts with base-rates when loss-aversion framing distorts assessment of objective frequencies by overweighting vivid potential losses.
structured_tensions.source_quote: prospect-theory conflicts with base-rates (base rates) when loss-aversion framing distorts assessment of objective frequencies by overweighting vivid potential losses.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Prospect-theory conflicts with anchoring when reference-point dependence means the same objective outcome is evaluated differently depending on the arbitrary anchor that sets the gain/loss boundary.
structured_tensions.source_quote: prospect-theory conflicts with anchoring (anchoring) when reference-point dependence means the same objective outcome is evaluated differently depending on the arbitrary anchor that sets the gain/loss boundary.

curation_notes.summary: The retained relation surface stays narrow around framing, reference points, and the descriptive-versus-normative boundary.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader System 1 or theory-of-mind expansion because those targets would widen the current vocabulary surface.
open_questions: The source is clearer on ally and antagonist pressure than on explicit current-vocabulary tension blocks, so structured tensions stay omitted.
